body: PT CIMB Niaga Finance (BNGA) recorded IDR 9.96 trillion in new financing in 2024, an 11.4% growth from the previous year, with total assets reaching IDR 10.53 trillion, up 34% year-over-year, and profit before tax increasing 12% to IDR 583 billion. The company aims to achieve IDR 9.5 trillion in new financing in 2025, focusing on digitalization and information technology development, including the implementation of digital innovations such as the CNAF mobile app and SCMS to support business operations.
title: CIMB Niaga Finance records 11.4% growth in new financing to IDR 9.96 trillion in 2024.

body: Indonesia's property market has seen a resurgence, with eight new projects launched in Jakarta and surrounding areas in the second half of 2024, driven by strong demand for commercial properties like shophouses, particularly from the food and beverages sector. Major players such as PT Ciputra Development Tbk (CTRA) and PT Bumi Serpong Damai Tbk (BSDE) have capitalized on this opportunity, with PT Ciputra Development Tbk reaching 55% of its annual targets in the first half of 2024, while PT Bumi Serpong Damai Tbk emerged as the market leader in shophouse sales.
title: Indonesia's property market sees resurgence in second half of 2024 with new project launches and increased demand for commercial properties.

body: PT Multi Nice Paper Indonesia (MNPI), a joint venture between PT Multi Media Internasional Tbk (MMIX) and Fujian Nicepaper Hygiene Products Co., aims to contribute IDR 146 billion, or 43.84%, to MMIX's projected sales of IDR 333 billion by 2025. MNPI's nappy factory, with a production capacity of 72,000 nappies per day, is expected to start mass production in the second semester of 2025, with MMIX holding a 15% stake in the company.
title: PT Multi Nice Paper Indonesia targets IDR 146 billion in sales by 2025 with new nappy factory in Tangerang.

body: PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk (UNVR) has officially transferred its ice cream business, PT Magnum Ice Cream Indonesia, in a deal approved by 77.9% of independent shareholders, expected to raise IDR 7 trillion. The company plans to distribute the net proceeds from the divestment as cash dividends to shareholders, although details are yet to be announced, with the next dividend distribution likely in mid-July.
title: Unilever Indonesia transfers ice cream business to PT Magnum Ice Cream Indonesia, expected to raise IDR 7 trillion.

body: Morgan Stanley Co International Plc (MS) sold 188.75 million shares of PT MD Pictures Tbk, reducing its stake to 11.51%, while other foreign investors, including HSBC-Fund SVS A/C Chengdong Investment Corp-Self and CGS International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd, also made notable sales in various Indonesian companies. Meanwhile, domestic investors, such as PT Maybank Sekuritas Indonesia and individual investors like Lo Kheng Hong, made significant purchases, increasing their stakes in companies like PT Gajah Tunggal Tbk and PT Armada Berjaya Trans Tbk.
title: Morgan Stanley and other foreign and domestic investors make notable changes to their stakes in various Indonesian companies.

body: PT Hartadinata Abadi Tbk (HRta) is set to offer Phase II of its Sustainable Bonds II 2025, valued at IDR 100 billion, with a fixed interest rate of 7.60% per annum and a 3-year maturity, aiming to raise IDR 1 trillion. The bond proceeds will be used primarily for working capital to expand its gold jewellery and bullion manufacturing business, with PT Bahana Sekuritas as lead underwriter and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk as trustee.
title: PT Hartadinata Abadi to Issue IDR 100 Billion Sustainable Bonds to Expand Gold Jewellery Business

body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) and other major Indonesian banks, including PT Bank Mandiri Tbk (BMRI) and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), are expected to experience attractive growth opportunities in 2025, driven by their strong fundamentals and successful navigation of market challenges in 2024. PT Ina Sekuritas Indonesia has issued buy recommendations for BMRI and BBRI shares, citing their solid growth prospects, while BBCA shares received an add recommendation with a price target of IDR 11,200 due to its slower CASA growth and cautious approach.
title: Indonesian Banking Giants Expected to Experience Attractive Growth Opportunities in 2025

body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange temporarily suspended trading of PT Citra Putra Realty Tbk (CLAY) shares due to low liquidity over the past three months, with average daily transaction values below IDR 5 million and volumes fewer than 10,000 shares. The company's transaction volume and value have significantly decreased, from 100 shares worth IDR 11,100 in three months prior to 347,300 shares worth IDR 104.72 billion on Wednesday, with the stock closing at IDR 302.
title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Halts Trading of PT Citra Putra Realty TBK Due to Low Liquidity.

body: PT Perintis Triniti Properti Tbk (TRIN) reported a marketing revenue of IDR 1.89 trillion in 2024, an 81% growth from its initial target, driven primarily by its flagship project Sequoia Hills Sentul, which accounted for 42% of the total revenue. The company is optimistic about targeting IDR 1.8 trillion in marketing revenue for 2025, supported by a favorable outlook in the property industry, pro-business government policies, and post-pandemic economic recovery.
title: Triniti Land Reports 81% Growth in Marketing Revenue, Targets IDR 1.8 Trillion for 2025

body: PT RMK Energy Tbk's subsidiary, PT Truba Bara Banyu Enim, reported that its Q4 2024 exploration activities were hindered by land acquisition issues, resulting in only partial realization of drilling plans, with a coring target of 1,101 meters and non-coring target of 4,359 meters. The company plans to continue exploration in Q1 2025 with a drilling budget of IDR1.401 billion, aiming to drill 29 points at a total depth of 3,540 meters in Block - Lekukam and Block B - Pinang.
title: PT RMK Energy's Subsidiary Releases Q4 2024 Exploration Report, Faces Land Acquisition Issues.
